name: The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas

content.verdict: The Ocean Club is the grown-up's grown-up Bahamas hotel — a 35-acre former Huntington Hartford colonial estate where 107 rooms unfold across the Hartford Wing, Crescent Wing, and a handful of private cottages. Versailles Gardens, a 12th-century French cloister, two and a half kilometers of sugar-white beach, and Dune by Jean-Georges make it the most quietly luxurious address on Paradise Island.
content.best_room: Hartford Wing rooms come with full butler service and the most direct beach access — request an Ocean View King with a wraparound balcony. For a once-in-a-lifetime stay, the Beachfront Cottages are private hideaways with plunge pools and dedicated butlers, set among palm gardens at the water's edge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and Versailles Gardens
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private transfer from Nassau, butler-led check-in at the Hartford Wing, late afternoon walk through the seven-terrace Versailles Gardens up to the 12th-century cloister. Sunset cocktails on the Library terrace, dinner at Dune by Jean-Georges.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach day with butler service
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow morning on the resort's signature stretch of white sand. Butler-arranged cabana with chilled towels, fruit plates, and frozen drinks. Lunch at the Beach Bar, afternoon nap, dinner at Ocean Club Dining Room.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa morning and Nassau evening
itinerary_7_nights.description: Couples Watsu treatment in the open-air Balinese-inspired spa villa. Light lunch at the spa garden, afternoon by the adult pool. Evening crossing into Nassau for dinner at Graycliff and a tour of its historic wine cellar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sailing the Exumas
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day private catamaran charter through the nearby cays — swimming with the famous Exuma pigs, snorkeling at Thunderball Grotto, lunch on board. Return for sunset cocktails on your terrace and casual dinner at Dune.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tennis, golf, or do nothing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Optional morning round at the Ocean Club Golf Course (Tom Weiskopf, ranked among the Caribbean's best) or court time at the resort's tennis facility. Afternoon at the beach with butler service. Dinner at the Ocean Club Dining Room with the Library after-dinner aperitif.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private dining and gardens
itinerary_7_nights.description: Couples breakfast in Versailles Gardens. Spend the day moving between beach, pool, and the cloister. Sunset Champagne in the gardens, then a private dinner for two on the cloister terrace, candlelit, butler-served.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow morning and farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Breakfast in your room, final stroll along the beach, late checkout in the Hartford Wing. Light lunch poolside, then private transfer back to Nassau International.

caveats: The Ocean Club is on Paradise Island — you'll see the Atlantis silhouette from parts of the property, although the estate itself feels a world apart.
Service is famously gracious but quiet; if you want all-day pool DJs and party energy, this isn't that hotel.
Rates are at the top of the Bahamas market — expect $1,500+ all-in even on shoulder season dates.

faqs.question: Hartford Wing or Crescent Wing — which is better?
faqs.answer: Hartford is the original wing, closer to the beach, smaller, with butler service included. Crescent rooms are larger and more contemporary. Honeymooners almost always prefer Hartford.

faqs.question: Is butler service really included?
faqs.answer: Yes in the Hartford Wing and the Cottages — your butler handles unpacking, dinner reservations, beach setup, in-room dining, and pretty much anything else. Crescent rooms share a concierge team.

faqs.question: Can we eat at Dune every night?
faqs.answer: Most couples eat at Dune two or three nights — it's the signature Jean-Georges room with ocean views. Ocean Club Dining Room is the more formal alternative, plus there's the Library and Beach Bar for casual nights.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, families do stay here, but the property attracts a high share of couples and the atmosphere is consistently calm and adult-oriented.

faqs.question: What's the best time to go?
faqs.answer: Late November to mid-December and mid-April to early June give you the best mix of warm water, sunshine, and lower rates. Avoid late August through October for hurricane risk.

faqs.question: How long is the transfer from Nassau airport?
faqs.answer: About 35 minutes including the bridge crossing — the resort arranges private cars on request.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort, Bahamas

Hello,

We are planning our honeymoon and would like to enquire about a 7-night stay at The Ocean Club in a Hartford Wing Ocean View King.

Could you please confirm:
- Honeymoon package amenities (Champagne, in-room dining credit, spa credit, room upgrade if available)
- Butler service inclusions in Hartford
- Versailles Gardens private dinner availability and pricing
- Spa couples treatment availability
- Private airport transfer pricing from Lynden Pindling International

Thank you,
content.extra_notes: The Ocean Club's Versailles Gardens are open to overnight guests around the clock — a candlelit walk through the cloister at night is one of the property's signature honeymoon moments. Dune dinner reservations open with your booking; book the first or last night before you arrive.
